name: Classic Potato Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Matzo meal, or as needed
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the potatoes.
2. Squeeze out as much moisture as possible.
3. Combine the grated potatoes, onion, eggs, matzo meal, salt, pepper, and sugar in a bowl.
4. Puree half of this mixture in a food processor or blender, then recombine it with the remaining mixture.
5. Add more matzo meal if needed to make a thick batter.
6. Heat oil in a heavy frying pan (about 1/8 inch deep).
7. Drop portions of the mixture into the hot oil using a large spoon.
- For thicker pancakes, leave the batter mounded high.
- For thinner, flatter disks with crisp edges, flatten the batter with a spoon as soon as it hits the oil.
8. Cook until golden brown on one side (about 4 minutes), then turn and cook the other side until golden brown.
9. Remove latkes to paper towels to drain excess oil briefly before serving.
10. Accompany hot latkes with sour cream, yogurt, and/or applesauce.
Sage latkes: Season with ½ teaspoon crumbled dry sage leaves (or 1 tsp fresh) and ¼ cup chopped parsley. Fry in duck or goose fat.
Garlic rosemary latkes: Omit onion and add 2 cloves chopped garlic and 2 teaspoons chopped fresh rosemary. Cook in olive oil and serve with a sprinkle of salt instead of sour cream.
Leek latkes: Omit onion and substitute julienned leeks.
Brussels sprouts: Add 6 coarsely grated Brussels sprouts to the potato batter. Serve with sour cream seasoned with a dash of Dijon or whole-seed mustard. Do not let Brussels sprout latkes sit too long; they become bitter.
